calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Hearing On Request For Default Judgment; Request For Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-02-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant YORKPRO, a division of York Risk Service Group, Inc.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Plaintiff Tom McMillin was present in pro per, placed under oath and testified.
Russell Robinson, a witness for, and former attorney of plaintiff Tom McMillin, was placed under oath and testified.
Both Messrs. McMillin and Robinson appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendant YORKPRO, a division of York Risk Service Group, Inc.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract and Conversion was filed on March 15, 2015 and personally served on defendant YORKPRO's, a division of York Risk Service Group, Inc., Agent of Process on July 14, 2015.
Default was entered on October 27, 2015.
Default as to defendant YORKPRO, a division of York Risk Service Group, Inc. was entered on August 27, 2015.
Order dismissing the case pursuant to CRC 3.1385 was filed on August 7, 2019.
Order granting plaintiff's motion to set-aide the dimissal was entered on January 10, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed on January 21, 2021.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Tom McMillin, and against the defaulted defendant YORKPRO, a division of York Risk Service Group, Inc., as follows:

Damages/Principal - $90,000.00.
Prejudgment Interest - $49,866.95.
Costs - $530.00.

Total - $140,396.95.

Plaintiff to prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-17-2021 Continued To Apr-20-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Hearing On Request For Default Judgment; Request For Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-21-2021 Continued To Mar-02-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant YORKPRO, a division of York Risk Services Group, Inc.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Plaintiff Tom McMillin was present in pro per, placed under oath and testified.
Russell B. Robinson, the former attorney of, and witness for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
Both Messrs. McMillin and Robinson appeared via ZOOM for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by defendant YORKPRO, a division of York Risk Services Group, Inc.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract and Conversion was filed on March 5, 2015, and personally served on defendant YORKPRO's Agent of Process on July 14, 2015.
Default as to defendant YORKPRO, a division of York Risk Services Group, Inc., was entered on October 27, 2015.
Order DISMISSING plaintiff's case pursuant to CRC 3.185 was filed on August 7, 2019.
Order GRANTING plaintiff's motion to set-aside the dismissal was filed on January 10, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed today, January 21, 2021.

Plaintiff Tom McMillin submits the following exhibit for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Confidential Settlement Agreement & Mutual Release (Undated/Unsigned)......ID/EVID

Good cause appearing, the Court CONTINUES this matter to March 2, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. for further default prove-up hearing, and in order for plaintiff to provide the Court with additional information in support of his request for Court Judgment.

Plaintiff to provide defendant(s) with notice of the continued hearing date. Plaintiff STIPULATES to the return of the exhibit.

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiff To Set Aside Dismissal; Memorandum; Declaration
Off Calendar Jan-10-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, or objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF TOM MCMILLIN's Motion By Plaintiff To Set Aside Dismissal as follows:

Plaintiff Tom McMillin's unopposed motion to set aside dismissal is granted.

Order signed after hearing.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
